items.prompt: When an R Markdown document is compiled, it primarily:
choices: Executes embedded code and inserts results into a synchronized report
Deletes all dataset versions from DVC remote storage
Converts HIPAA data into unrestricted open licenses
Replaces FAIR Interoperable formats with screenshots only
items.explain: Section 13.10 describes compiling R Markdown so code, narrative, and outputs stay coupled.

items.prompt: DVC is useful when training data and model weights are too large to track with Git alone.
items.explain: The chapter positions DVC for large datasets and models with pointers in Git and bytes in external storage.

items.prompt: Example 13.44 uses DVC to track which research artifacts?
choices: Training data, model weights, and evaluation results
items.explain: Example 13.44 explicitly tracks training data, model weights, and evaluation results.

items.prompt: Name the three stages in the DVC pipeline highlighted in Example 13.45.
items.rubric: Accept preprocess / preprocessing, train / training, and evaluate / evaluation (order may vary slightly).

items.prompt: A versioned DVC pipeline lets researchers regenerate results with a single pipeline run instead of a fragile manual script sequence.
items.explain: Example 13.45 emphasizes defining and versioning each step so regeneration is a single pipeline run.
